segment-text: Shooting script 20 May 2013

* This script is shorter than it looks because much of the fast, naturalistic dialogue will involve interruptions, people talking over each other, simultaneous dialogue (etc.) * Anything in bold & italics is an improvisation. Sometimes we have written out dialogue to give a feel of what kind of improv it could be, sometimes we have just given a general description.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LANDING - DAY

We open on a grey background, which gradually gains focus and texture. We are also becoming aware of a muddled soup of sound - distant voice, thumping footsteps, distorted music from a TV somewhere. Now the grey background is no longer filling the frame. We realize that is a low-shot of a stone. Suddenly, the stone rolls alarmingly towards camera, making us jump. A small 4-year-old girl - JESS - is rolling the stone down an upstairs corridor.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LOUNGE - DAY

The air is filled with the heroic chords from the theme to the 1958 Kirk Douglas movie "The Vikings". We are on a C/U of a wide-eyed, 6-year-old, transported boy. He is sitting on the back of a sofa, totally rapt, as on the TV lots of Vikings fight an epic battle. We can hear his mother calling in the distance. Mi-ckey!
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - VARIOUS - DAY
C/U, fast-cut, rapid-fire succession of plugs being pulled from sockets.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LANDING - DAY
JESS, is, with some difficulty, continuing to roll her grey stone along the floor.
We should have left two hours ago! (etc)
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - HALL - DAY
Fish food is being dropped into a fish tank by a rather serious-looking 10-year-old LOTTIE.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY INT. MILK-BOTTLES - DAY
C/U of a cluster of clinking empty milk bottles being carried towards the front door, one of the bottles has a note on it.
I'm just saying, that's all (etc)
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LOUNGE - DAY
MICKEY is still hypnotised by the Vikings.
(MICKEY remains transfixed as someone's midriff crosses the
(Half turns) Eh? Wha-?
He overbalances and plummets backwards, landing with a thump behind the sofa.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - CUPBOARD - DAY
A finger is stabbing at the numbers 1066 on a keypad.
(Puzzled) Battle of Hastings isn't working. No, that's too guessable, I changed it.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - BACK DOOR - DAY
C/U rapid-fire sequence of bolts being latched. LOTTIE is doing this.
So what is it now? Er, I'll do that, you do the herding.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LANDING - DAY
For the first time, we see the face of an adult - DOUG - as he comes up the stairs, chuntering to himself.
And we've hit the rush-hour, just as I...
He spots JESS on the landing, with her bag, he turns very cheery and positive.
Come on, Jessypie, we need to get a move on, eh? Scottish Grandad and his puppies are waiting for us. Come on, I'll carry your... (He picks
up her bag and nearly gives himself a triple hernia) Je-eez! What the (He looks inside the bag. We see the large stone that she was
That's Eric. Eric.
Yes he wants to come to Scotland too.
DOUG hesitates for a moment, not sure how to play this one.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - HALL - DAY
With a clump - clump - clump, ABI, the Mum, is bustling along the hall carrying three bags, as we hear MICKEY still fighting imaginary Vikings.
A piercing car alarm goes off.
That's our car alarm! (to herself as she ferrets for her car keys.) Yes! Thank you Sherlock!
She bumps and bustles to the front door and tries to turn off the alarm with the fob, but it won't co-operate.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LANDING - DAY
DOUG is trying to sweet-talk JESS, it feels like he's walking on eggshells - very fragile eggshells.
The thing is darling, Eric is quite heavy and why don't you take one of your lighter friends like - hang on (He has delved further into the bag) Wha - there's...?
(Looking into the bag) That's Norman.
That's ... half a breezeblock. How the hell did you - (He starts to take the breezeblock out of the bag)
I can't get to sleep without Norman.
... You sleep with a breezeblock in your bed.

No, that'd be stupid. Only the soft stones go in my bed. Norman goes under my bed. He stops the bad stones getting in. I can't sleep without him, he - (Jess explains why she has to take all the rocks to Scotland despite DOUG'S
INT/EXT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - DOORSTEP - DAY
LOTTIE watches her frazzled Mum trying to turn off the car alarm.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - MICKEY'S ROOM - DAY
MICKEY charges in and starts gathering up his favourite Viking action toys and pockets them, along with a short length of rope. A huge poster of Odin covers one wall.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - LANDING - DAY
DOUG is trying to draw a line in the sand.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
No I'm sorry, sweetheart, but you can't take Norman to Scotland - or Eric.
Right. Then I'm going to hold my breath.

(Suddenly very alarmed) No, don't do that. (She's already started) Jess? Jess, stop that! ... Jess, you... OK, fine, they can come, it's OK, look, see? ... They're coming. (He is stuffing Eric and Norman back in the bag.)
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - MICKEY'S ROOM -DAY

MICKEY is rapidly hunting - he finds a toy sword. The car alarm goes off again outside, ABI exclaims, upstairs JESS yells, it's a wall of noise, which peaks when MICKEY raises his toy sword triumphantly above his head, with a cry of "For Odin!" and then hurtles towards the camera shouting "CH-A-A-ARGE!"
EXT. HIGHLAND LOCH - DAY

Hard cut to the silence of a loch. Mist swirls over the water and the mountains beyond. On the loch is a small boat, with a figure sitting in it, an elderly man in his seventies with a fishing rod. He is scruffy and unshaven, but has a style and presence. This is

We linger a little to take in the silence, the grandeur, the tiny figure in the boat. The idyll is punctuated by the trill of a mobile phone. GORDIE lets out a weary groan, he lobs the blanket over it, muffling the sound.
EXT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - DAY
ABI is throwing a football and an armful of teddies into the back seat of the family car. DOUG is loading up the bags.
So, her best friends are stones now.
(Adjusting the child seat) Not all of them, some of them are bricks.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Well, have you... y'know... (Still fighting the child seat) Have I what?
Have you spoken to anyone about it? What, like a geologist? (Still struggling with the car seat) This thing hates me!
I'm just saying this... obsessive collecting is rather abnormal behaviour for a child to ABI (Laden with sarcasm) Oh she's behaving abnormally is she? I wonder why.
Oh for Christ's sake, Abi, just They discover that LOTTIE is standing right behind them, and
instantly snap into smiley mode.
OK, sweetie?
Everything alright?
I just need a list. ... A list?
Yes, a list of the lies we have to tell. In case I forget one.
DOUG and ABI look at each other.
EXT. HIGHLAND LOCH - DAY

Water laps gently against the side of GORDIE'S boat as he fishes on the loch. His face is a picture of contentment, until he catches a glimpse of something. At the far end of the loch a shiny Range Rover comes over a rise and then disappears. It is heading his way. GORDIE mutters something - probably obscene.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Cut a big w/s of the boat on the loch. GORDIE is a tiny figure. Something big runs very quickly across frame in the foreground. Was that an ostrich? Surely not.
INT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - HALL - DAY
ABI enters at speed, with LOTTIE dogging her steps. MICKEY has his length of rope and is practising tying knots.
That's a clove hitch (etc)

ABI disappears deeper into the house. LOTTIE takes the responsibility of changing MICKEY's pullover, which he's put on the wrong way round. Outside, DOUG is trying to close the jam-packed boot of the car.
Why aren't we flying to Scotland?
Because the volcano in Iceland has erupted again which means -it's too expensive
I like planes. Planes have films in the seats. And ladies with lots of teeth who bring you stuff. And they have those men who take away your toothpaste.
ABI returns, hurrying up the hall, calling into the house while hunting for her keys.
Keys... keys.
Now DOUG is approaching from the car, checking his phone.
M25's up the spout.
A list would be really helpful, so that-
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Look, it's perfectly simple, darling, it's Grandad's 75th birthday party and we don't want to
upset him because he's been a bit poorly, so JESS
There is just the briefest flicker of an awkward look between DOUG and ABI.
Yes that's right, but - (The car
alarm goes off again.) Je-sus H.
You're not supposed to shout Jesus's name like that.
(Hunting for the fob) It's alright, darling, I'm just asking for his help.
To stop the car alarm? Yes.
Coincidentally, the car alarm stops itself.
... See? (Looks up) Thank you, Jesus.
JESS is looking at her suspiciously.
Come on, all aboard!
The little ones race for the car and start climbing in.
INT/EXT. DOUG AND ABI'S HOUSE - DOORSTEP - DAY
ABI and DOUG come out.
(Pointedly) No ... I gave you my key... remember?
Oh... yeh... well, um, I -
We can't leave the house unlocked. Yes, I am aware of LOTTIE (by the car) Don't start. We're not starting, darling, you go supervise the little ones, that'd be great.
(Sudden thought) Jess hasn't had Kelvin round to play, has she? (Abi's face drops and he realises the answer is yes) Oh for Kelvin, the key-hider. We agreed, never again. (They start to argue.)
(Calling from the car) Don't start! (Calling back) Nobody's starting, sweetheart.(DOUG heads off) Where are you- ?
(Calling from the car) Mu-um! She won't let me put her seatbelt on!
ABI is approaching the car to sort it out. JESS is sitting with her piece of breezeblock next to her.
I've told her she'll be thrown through the windscreen and cut into tiny pieces when you crash. Yes, thank you, Mickey.
I don't like seatbelts, they're stupid (etc.)
(Brightly) I know, why don't we ask Eric if he'll -
That's Norman.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
JESS looks at her.
...He's a breezeblock. He's got no arms, so how could he possibly ABI (Her patience snaps) I'm putting it on. Noooo!

It all kicks off. LOTTIE tells everyone to 'keep calm'. ABI tells LOTTIE to stay out of it, but all this is in the background, as we find DOUG who is now standing on next door's doorstep. Their neighbour LUCY opens the door.
Oh hi Lucy... um, Abi's managed to lose her keys somewhere... and I need to lock up, so could I just borrow the spare?
He holds his hand out, but there's a wary embarrassment in Lucy's eyes.
(Perplexed)... If you could just give me the sp- (He realizes that she's been instructed not to hand him any keys)... Oh.
LUCY looks hesitantly towards ABI for reassurance. It takes ABI a moment to register what's happened.
(Calls) Oh no, it's OK, Luce, it's
Oh right. (Relieved, she takes the keys off a hook) So... you're off on your travels.
(V. hacked off) Yes... to visit my Dad.
(Wearing a painted smile) Oh right, so... you're driving all the way to Scotland.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
...That's right... Yes.
... Together.
(Too quick) Yes.
EXT. MOTORWAY - DAY
The roar of traffic announces a big W/S as the family car heads past a sign for THE NORTH. We can hear DOUG and ABI talking over each other. DOUG is driving.
We cannot drive to the Highlands in one day.
INT. CAR - DAY
MICKEY is practising his knot-tying in the back.
I told Gavin we'd be there by tonight.
We haven't a cat's hope inDOUG We can share the driving. What, with Jenson Button? Look-
Please don't argue.
We're not arguing, darling, we're discussing.
This is how it starts. You start off discussing, then you end up shouting and screaming.
(Quiet, looking out of the window) ... And then the policeman comes.
DOUG and ABI share a guilty look.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
... That was just a misunderstanding, sweetheart.
EXT - MOTORWAY
Car still heading up the outside lane.
Sometimes when grown-ups... discuss things... very loudly... people get the wrong idea.
He let me play with his tazor.
Well... he didn't let you.
EXT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - DAY
So, we're all going to have a little holiday together?
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - LADIES' TOILET - DAY
LOTTIE is waiting outside a cubicle, listening anxiously to the conversation that's going on inside, between ABI and
That's right, it'll be lovely, won't it?
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - CUBICLE - DAY
JESS is sitting on the toilet with ABI in attendance.
So does that mean Daddy's coming back to live with us again?
No, sweetheart, we've been through this, haven't we, eh, and the important thing to remember is that Mummy and Daddy...
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - GENTS' TOILET - DAY

(To MICKEY)...both love you very much, but sometimes a Mummy and Daddy reach a point where, well, things change, and one of them finds they don't really love the other one like they used to, and then maybe, because of that, the other one makes a mistake.
(Finishing off at urinal) Like Lloyd's Dad?
Well... that was quite a big mistake... running over Lloyd's Mum with their car... that was - I'm talking about a smaller mistake, where someone does something that is...
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - CUBICLE - DAY
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - GENTS' TOILET - DAY
(Now doing up the Velcro tags on MICKEY'S shoes)...one of them goes to someone called a solicitor, and sometimes, if one of them is getting angry...
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - CUBICLE - DAY
Cut to LOTTIE outside the door, who quietly joins in.
Very much and always will... Cut back to ABI inside cubicle. ...they just keep getting crosser and crosser..
INT. MOTORWAY SERVICE STATION - GENTS' TOILET - DAY
Doug pulls on the Velcro tags with increasing vigour, the anger really bleeding through
...until the Mummy and Daddy start blaming each other for every tiny thing even though none of this would be happening if the other one had just listened to him in the first place.
He finishes doing up the Velcro tags
Yes, Mickey?
(in pain) These are a bit tight.
EXT. HIGHLAND LOCH - DAY

The Range Rover GORDIE was watching earlier skids to a halt, beside the loch. A brisk, busy, crisply dressed man of about 40 gets out with a hesitant woman hidden behind a lot of knitwear - his wife, MARGARET.
(mutters) Chase me round the loch on water skis why don't you...
(calls) Dad! Da-ad! We've brought
response)...Only you left them behind!(still no response)... And it's twelve o'clock!
(Calling back) Leave them on the shore by the dead sheep.
GAVIN and MARGARET look at each other, a little thrown. In the boat GORDIE starts to swear, mutter and gesticulate.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
(As he heads back to the car) With his heart he's supposed to stay calm. I mean, how can anybody get so worked up about fishing?
In the boat GORDIE is looking down at something on the seat at the other end. He's got a tiny TV with him and he is watching the racing.
EXT. A MISTY HILLTOP - DAY
Five fearsome Vikings are charging, axes raised to strike, towards the camera. It's impressive and terrifying.
INT. CAR - DAY
MICKEY wakes up. It takes him a moment to come round.
...Is this Scotland?
EXT. MOTORWAY - DAY
The car is stationary in a traffic jam.
INT. CAR - DAY
ABI is at the wheel, DOUG is asleep. The radio is carrying various reports of rioting in English cities.
When Dad wakes up, are you going to go 'I told you so' about the traffic congestion?
No, darling.
The car moves forward at a crawl. JESS pokes DOUG hard. He lurches awake.
She's not going to say, 'I told you so'.
Wha...? Je-ss! Why did you...(He sees the traffic)
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
It's not polite to just wake someone up like that.
That's right. God. Look at this lot.
ABI's phone rings and from force of habit DOUG reaches for it. Leave it. DOUG answers it anyway.
(He answers) Abi's phone... (To ABI) It's Leon... I'll call him back.
She'll call you back.
DOUG looks hard at her, but says nothing.
I feel car-sick, can I sit in the front?
Next time we stop... if we're allowed to.
(Perplexed) We're stopped now.
The SAT NAV pipes up. SAT NAV In 50 metres, turn right.
We can't bloody turn right, 'cos there is no bloody right turn.
She can't hear you. She's a robot.
MICKEY and JESS start speculating about whether she's a really tiny robot - what if she turns evil, etc. DOUGIE's mobile rings.
Oh God... Gavin.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Doug, accept it, there's no way we're going to get there tonight.
(In denial) The traffic always gets better after the Leeds turnoff.(Answers phone) Hi Gavin... Tell him we won't make it today.
Yes it is safe me talking to you 'cos Abi's driving... Because in England we let women drive. Tell him...
No, we will make it tonight but probably quite late... OK (hangs up)
ABI stares at him. Continue straight ahead. CUT wide to the immovable traffic jam.
EXT. ROAD - DAY

Pouring rain. A dual carriageway. The car is on the hard shoulder. ABI and DOUG are outside. Though we can't hear them over the rain and the cars and lorries hammering by, they are obviously screaming at each other. Inside the car MICKEY is reading but LOTTIE and JESS's faces are pressed against the window watching. From the children's POV, we see their parents silently screaming and shouting as the rain pours down. MICKEY stops reading and watches as well.
EXT. GAVIN'S HOUSE - ESTABLISHER - DAY INT. GAVIN AND MARGARET'S HOUSE - DAY

MARGARET has a big planning board with all the tables on it and colour-coded name-tags for all the guests. GAVIN comes in talking on the phone, while updating the table-plans on his tablet. SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
(To phone) I wondered if Sir Donald was in? ...Thank you.
We've got three bulimics... Do you think we should put them next to the buffet or a long way from it?
Near the toilet's probably more important. But, do you know, I think Doreen would be... more comfortable away from the top
Well maybe we should let your Dad decide, because she is...
She peters out as GAVIN ignores her and moves the name tag to another table. He starts talking to the phone again.
Sir Donald... Gavin here... Gavin McLeod... I hear you finally got that hole-in-one. (etc) (He starts canvassing him about the captaincy of the golf club.)

KENNETH, their bookish 15-year-old son, walks in. MARGARET does not see him. There's a wasp buzzing up and down the window pane. MARGARET picks up a newspaper and whacks it. She hasn't killed it. She bashes it with the paper again... and again... and again and again in a furious assault. She stands there breathing heavily, but becomes aware of KENNETH standing, perplexed, watching her. She instantly brightens.
Well, he won't be bothering us again.
EXT. TOWN - DAY
A small pretty British town. A lot of hanging flowers, thatch and half timbering. The family come out of a hotel. JESS and MICKEY are arguing cheerfully over who was sick the most.
Jess, OK... but I never thought Mickey was going to be sick as well did you?
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Certainly not that much... And not that far... You may as well throw that shirt away.
Thank you for stopping for the night.
I... just want to get there as soon as possible. I know.
I quite like being sick... it's like being a fountain.
INT. GAVIN AND MARGARET'S HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
It's deserted. GORDIE puts his head around the door. He goes over to the planning board and peers at the top table where he is sitting.
Oh Jesus Christ No!
He takes a couple of names off it and puts them at distant tables, then thinks again and puts one of them in the bin.
EXT. TOWN - DAY
ABI and DOUG are walking down the street. Ahead of them, the kids are still arguing about whose sick had the most carrots in it. ABI stops, there's something on her mind.
Her phone rings. She quickly glances at who it is and then turns it off, as DOUG watches her. She regroups.
... why... why are we putting ourselves through all this? Maybe we should just tell your Dad the truth.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
No, no, it's too big a risk, it could really upset him, the idea of us... splitting... you're a big favourite of his... Well, he's a big favourite of mine, but...
We hear LOTTIE talking to MICKEY and JESS.
(To the KIDS) Look, let's just call it a draw... you both produced an enormous amount of sick.
Abi, please... he can't have long... let's just... let him have his party.
They manage to smile at each other.
INT. TOWN - CAFE - DUSK

The FAMILY sits at a table. We see them from across the cafe and for a moment they look like a happy family. JESS is joining up straws to make a fantastically long straw. MICKEY's in animated conversation with his Mum; LOTTIE with her Dad.
(to LOTTIE, over the babble) For the last time, Lottie, no one's going to ask you if Mum and me are living in the same house.
(taking out her notebook) OK.
Why are you writing that down?
So you can't say you didn't say it if someone does ask me.
LOTTIE looks at her watch and writes down the time and date.
(Turning to MICKEY and JESS) OK. Kids, just to remind everyone, what we're saying is that when we get to Scotland... nobody must tell anyone that me and Mum are living in different houses... OK?
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Can we tell them you accidentally walked in on the headmistress when she was doing a poo? Well...
Improv on what they can and can't say, which includes one of the little ones asking why they can't tell anyone about the parents living in separate houses.
It's lying.
It's not lying, it's... not mentioning something.
But what if someone asks me 'are your Mum and Dad getting divorced?'
Lottie, you're visiting your Grandad... you're not going on the Jeremy Kyle Show.
An animated discussion begins.
EXT. HILLTOP - DUSK An amazing, almost unnatural sunset.
KENNETH is with GORDIE, who is sitting in an old deck-chair. They are not entirely comfortable together. GORDIE offers KENNETH a beer, but he declines. KENNETH sounds 20 years older than he is.
Quite a sunset... (GORDIE nods) It's volcanic dust in the air refracting the low level sunlight.
Ah, I thought it was those new pills they'd given me... Do you want a couple of packets of the blue ones... I bet you could make a tidy profit selling them at school.
It's a joke, but KENNETH doesn't react.
Play something then...
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
KENNETH has his violin with him. He starts to play some classical music.
No, no, the proper stuff... I know you like it... Ah, Kenny, you need to cut loose...(KENNETH hesitates)... Go on, go mental.
...Dad says I need to concentrate on my exam pieces.
He resumes the classical piece. GORDIE quietly shakes his head.
INT. TOWN - CAFE - DUSK
They have just paid, and in the background, ABI is trying to stop JESS and MICKEY shouting out her pin number. LOTTIE is talking to her DAD.
So you'll tell Grandad about you and Mum splitting up when he's better...
(A little hesitant)Yeah... Yes.
And is there anything you've not told me about anything?
No, no... we've told you everything. LOTTIE gets out her book to write this down.
(He turns to ABI) I'm glad we stopped. It's actually quite nice here.
They come out a door into the high street.
EXT. TOWN - DUSK

They walk into a wall of noise. The pretty country town high street has transformed into an alcohol-fuelled Saturday night orgy of drinking, shouting and fighting. Fat men stripped to the waist, drunk girls on very long heels having slanging matches, police restraining a man, ambulance lights flashing (etc).
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
ABI, JESS and MICKEY join LOTTIE and DOUG. They stare in amazement and horror.
Is this another riot?
No, this is a Saturday night. (to the kids) OK... stick between me and Dad, and don't make eyecontact with anyone.

They compress into a protective huddle and start to move out of frame, but the appalled LOTTIE lingers a moment and is left alone in shot. DOUG's arm reaches into frame and pulls LOTTIE out of shot, just as a YOUNG WOMAN vomits copiously into the space where LOTTIE had been standing. Cut to further down the street as the family scuttle along the pavement towards the sanctuary of their hotel.
Come on kids, stay close (etc)
The kids are fascinated/appalled. We glimpse elements of the debauchery through their eyes, e.g. a woman swaying with her pants round her ankles, drunks being wrestled into police vans.
Is this what grown-ups do after we go to bed?
One drunk shouts at MICKEY.
What are you staring at?
... He's six. Sorry love, he's mixed cider with stupid.
They have now reached the entrance to their hotel.
That man and woman on the front of
that car... are they doing sex?
(as he ushers her inside) Not quite yet... give it another 30 seconds.
A car alarm goes off.
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
(as she disappears inside) Are car alarms to tell you when someone's doing sex on your car? (etc)
As they go inside, MICKEY is lagging behind to watch the debauchery.
(calling from inside) Come on, Mickey, there's nothing to see.
Yes there is, this is better than the zoo! Mick-ey!
MICKEY is ushered inside. DOUG and ABI exchange a half-amused shake of the head.
INT. TOWN - HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT.

DOUG is lying awake on a camp bed. Outside, the Saturday night mayhem sounds just as horrendous as before. He glances across to a bed where ABI is fast asleep, with JESS curled up next to her. DOUG feels a sense of loss, excluded. He looks across to the sleeping LOTTIE, who's on a camp-bed. The bedlam outside gets louder, cries of 'Leave him, he's not worth it!' (etc) A blue police light can be seen flashing, as DOUG stares disconsolately at the ceiling. He will not sleep tonight.
EXT. ROAD - DAY
Hard cut to their car going along a straight section of dual carriageway.
INT. CAR - DAY

ABI's in the back with LOTTIE and MICKEY. All three are asleep. DOUG is driving. He has fallen asleep at the wheel. JESS sits in the front seat next to him. She calmly has one hand on the steering wheel keeping the car steady. The SAT NAV speaks. SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY At the next junction turn left. JESS wrinkles her brow. She can't remember which is left. Left?
But she goes to move the steering wheel. DOUG's eyes flicker open. He realises he's driving and jerks awake. Jesus!
Everyone wakes up. JESS smiles at him.
Why didn't you wake me up?
You said it was rude...
Not when I'm driving! (Waking) What's happening?
Daddy didn't like me driving while he was asleep. (etc)
EXT. ROAD - DAY
What?! You were asleep! (etc)

W/S OF THE FAMILY CAR PROGRESSING AS THIS DIALOGUE CONTINUES. We see the car turn left and a W/S develops into a spectacular mountainous vista, as the family head into the heart of the Highlands. Music starts to swell.
Is this Scotland?
You actually fell asleep?
Not really. (Changing subject) These are the Highlands, Mickey.
How high are the Highlands?
SHOOTING SCRIPT - 20 MAY
Couple of thousand feet.
How high is that in elephants? (etc) They are disappearing into the hills.
The car is speeding along a scenic coastal road. It turns left, away from the sea.
EXT. GAVIN AND MARGARET'S HOUSE - DAY House out in the wilds (or semi-wild)

You can see across to a loch and the spectacular mountains beyond. It's about as far away from London as you can get. DOUG drives their car up a track and parks. GAVIN and MARGARET come out to meet them. The CHILDREN tumble out of the car. This is all being watched from a window in the old house.
INT. GAVIN AND MARGARET'S HOUSE - BATHROOM - DAY

GORDIE watches out of the bathroom window. He looks dreadful... pale and sweating. His head ducks down and there is the sound of vomiting. He comes back up. The children are running, laughing across the lawn, shouting "Where are the puppies?"
INT. GAVIN AND MARGARET'S HOUSE - ENTRANCE HALL - DAY
Everyone except GORDIE is there.
So Mickey... belated birthday
present. (MARGARET hands him a
Is it a bicycle?
